methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Rain AI is a Series A chip design company founded by Gordon Wilson, currently valued at $500M post-money after raising $146M. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 8 private peers in Chips & Semiconductors at the same stage—including Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), and Substrate ($1.0B)—plus 3 public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two methods triangulate the valuation. Stage-Progression applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier to Rain's $500M last post-money valuation, implying $1.0B. Peer-Median anchors to the three same-sector, same-stage private comparables, which show a median valuation of $2.0B and a range spanning $1.0B to $5.0B. Public-Comp Anchoring using the sector's 24.2x median P/S multiple is inapplicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue. 

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $850M to $2.3B, with a median of $1.5B. This implies Rain AI's current $500M valuation sits materially below the peer cohort median, reflecting either a valuation discount relative to peers or a divergence in growth trajectory and capital efficiency assumptions. Key uncertainties include the dispersion within the peer set (driven partly by Etched's $5.0B outlier valuation) and the lack of revenue visibility to ground market-based multiples.
